title: update-two-cluster-verification
summary: Updated `scripts/verify-two-cluster.sh`'s auth section to authenticate via `auth/jwt-vso` (JWT/OIDC) with a positive `vso-demo`/audience-`vault` login, mandatory wrong-audience and wrong-service-account negative logins, and a strict claim-binding role review, plus an updated final summary; updated its static test suite to match, and verified everything live end-to-end.
keyLearnings: - Negative-login pattern under `set -euo pipefail`: `if kubectl_vault exec ... vault write ... jwt="${WRONG_JWT}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then fail_section ...; fi` — commands used as an `if` condition are exempt from `set -e`, so this correctly treats "login unexpectedly succeeded" as the failure branch without needing `|| true` tricks.

- Role/config strict-binding check reads `auth/${VSO_JWT_AUTH_MOUNT}/role/${VSO_JWT_AUTH_ROLE}` via `vault read -format=json | jq` and compares `bound_audiences`/`bound_subject` exactly against `${VSO_JWT_AUDIENCE}` and `system:serviceaccount:${VSO_NAMESPACE}:vso-demo` — proves the config, not just that a role exists.

- All minted JWT shell variables (`VSO_SA_JWT`, `WRONG_AUD_JWT`, `WRONG_SA_JWT`) are `unset` right after use and never echoed directly, per the "don't log full JWT values" requirement; only Vault's own login response is ever printed on failure.

- Task 07 (Makefile switch to `configure-vso-jwt-auth.sh` as default) had already landed in the working tree by the time this task ran even though it wasn't in this task's dependency list — worth checking `git log` before assuming a stale baseline in multi-task pipelines.

- Live-verified against the real two-cluster kind lab: both `bash scripts/verify-two-cluster.sh` and `make verify-two-cluster` pass all 7 sections including both negative auth checks; baseline secret (`larry`) confirmed restored after rotation. All 12 `scripts/tests/test-*.sh` suites (35 assertions in this one) pass with zero regressions.
blockers: none — all acceptance/validation criteria for task 08 met (bash -n; static assertions for `auth/jwt-vso/login`
no JWT leakage; live e2e run with all auth/sync/rotation sections passing and baseline restored).
